reasoning:
FICO recently experienced a sharp 4-day surge (gaining roughly 7.5%) up to $1952.31, largely fueled by a very strong Q2 earnings beat on April 23 (revenue up 15%, B2B scoring up 31%). While the business fundamentals are excellent and analysts have high long-term price targets ($2200+), such rapid post-earnings spikes often experience short-term profit-taking and mean reversion. 

The statistical trend analysis (which considers linear predictions and seasonality) indicates only a ~40% probability of remaining above 1952 next week (by May 4). Thus, the short-term forecast reflects this likely slight pullback.

As the forecast horizon increases, the short-term mean-reversion effect diminishes, and the stock's underlying strength (reiterated double-digit full-year growth) plus general market positive drift takes over. The stock is still significantly below its December 2024 high of ~$2332. Consequently, probabilities steadily increase toward and slightly past 0.5 for the 3-to-6-month timeframes, reflecting typical long-term baseline stock market returns and bringing the distance to 0.5 closer in alignment with maximized long-term uncertainty.
